name: Kung Pao Chicken
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces skinless, boneless chicken breast halves
a few dashes bottled hot pepper sauce
6 green onions (1 cup), cut into half-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chicken and pat it dry. Cut into 3/4 inch pieces.
2. In a medium bowl, stir together the chicken, sherry, and 1 teaspoon cornstarch. Let stand for 15 minutes.
3. For the sauce, in a small bowl, stir together water, soy sauce, 4 teaspoons cornstarch, sugar, vinegar, and bottled hot pepper sauce. Set aside.
4. Pour cooking oil into a wok or large skillet. Add more oil as necessary during cooking.
5. Preheat over medium-high heat. Stir-fry gingerroot and garlic in hot oil for 15 seconds.
6. Add the chicken mixture; stir-fry for 3 to 4 minutes, or until no pink remains.
7. Push the chicken from the center of the wok.
8. Stir sauce into the center of the wok.
9. Cook and stir till thickened and bubbly. Add green onion pieces and peanuts.
10. Stir all ingredients together to coat with sauce. Cook and stir for 1 to 2 minutes more, or until heated through.
11. Serve immediately with hot cooked rice. Garnish with green onion fans, if desired. Makes 4 servings.
